name: Puntal de l'Aljub (PR-CV 352) — Circular from Eslida
name_original: Puntal de l'Aljub (PR-CV 352) — Circular desde Eslida
region_intro: Hiking around Eslida offers access to the rugged and diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, the second-largest protected area in the Valencian Community. The region is characterized by its abrupt relief, featuring peaks, ridges, deep ravines, and valleys formed by unique sandstone outcrops. Extensive cork oak forests thrive here due to higher rainfall and specific soil conditions, creating distinctive woodlands. This varied topography provides a challenging yet rewarding environment for hikers. Best hiking trails around Eslida The most popular hiking route is Puntal de l'Aljub (PR-CV 352) — Circular from Eslida, a 7.4 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation changes and offers expansive views across the Sierra de Espadán. Another top favourite among local hikers is Eslida – Views of the Castle of Eslida loop from Eslida, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.5 km) path. This trail provides scenic views of the historic Castle of Eslida and surrounding landscapes. Local hikers also love the Eslida – Puntal de l'Aljub loop from Eslida, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain around Puntal de l'Aljub, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around Eslida is defined by its cork oak forests, sandstone outcrops, and abrupt relief with numerous peaks and ravines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Eslida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 350 hikers have used komoot to explore Eslida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Eslida?
A: Eslida, nestled within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, offers a diverse range of hiking trails. You'll find routes characterized by abrupt relief, unique sandstone outcrops, and extensive cork oak forests. The trails vary in difficulty, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents, catering to different experience levels. There are approximately 25 hiking routes available in the area.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Eslida?
A: Yes, Eslida offers several easier routes. While many trails feature significant elevation changes, there are at least 3 routes classified as easy. These are perfect for those looking for a less strenuous walk while still enjoying the natural beauty of the Sierra de Espadán.

Q: What are the most challenging hikes in the Eslida area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Eslida has approximately 4 difficult routes. These trails typically involve more significant elevation gains and rugged terrain, providing a rewarding experience amidst the park's dramatic landscapes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33831930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida – Ajuez Reservoir loop from Eslida&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 13 km with substantial elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Eslida?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Eslida are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877440627/puntal-de-l-aljub-pr-cv-352-circular-from-eslida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de l'Aljub (PR-CV 352) — Circular from Eslida&lt;/a&gt;, a 11.8 km loop offering expansive views and significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find trails with good viewpoints in Eslida?
A: Absolutely. The abrupt relief of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park means many trails offer stunning panoramic views. Routes often lead to peaks and mountain passes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10381847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida – Views of the Castle of Eslida loop from Eslida&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic vistas of the historic Castle of Eslida and the surrounding landscapes.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while hiking near Eslida?
A: The Eslida area is rich in both natural and historical landmarks. You can explore features like the unique cork oak forests and sandstone outcrops. Historically, the region boasts ancient villages and old hydraulic mills. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2756864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Castle&lt;/a&gt; and various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which offer both historical context and beautiful scenery.

Q: Is Eslida a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Eslida can be suitable for families, especially on its easier and moderate trails. The diverse landscape and historical elements can make for an engaging outing. It's recommended to choose routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances, such as some of the 3 easy routes available, to ensure a comfortable experience for all family members.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Eslida?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife. Be sure to check specific trail regulations or local signage for any restrictions, particularly during nesting seasons or in areas with livestock.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Eslida?
A: The Sierra de Espadán Natural Park experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are preferable, and winters are mild, though some higher sections might be cooler. The higher rainfall in the mountains contributes to the thriving cork oak forests.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Eslida?
A: The hiking trails around Eslida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique cork oak forests, the dramatic sandstone landscapes, and the varied terrain that offers both challenging ascents and rewarding viewpoints. The well-maintained network of trails also receives positive feedback.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the trailheads in Eslida?
A: While Eslida is a hub for many trails, public transport options directly to specific trailheads within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park can be limited. It's often most convenient to reach Eslida by car, and then access the trailheads from the village. For specific routes, checking local bus schedules to Eslida itself would be the first step.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Eslida?
A: Eslida village typically offers parking options for visitors. Many trails start directly from or near the village, making it convenient to park your vehicle before heading out. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas within Eslida to ensure proper access and avoid obstructing local residents.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through other interesting villages near Eslida?
A: Yes, the extensive network of trails in the Sierra de Espadán connects several charming villages. The GR36 long-distance path, for instance, traverses the park and passes through Eslida, linking it to other towns. A specific route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3969624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida – Aín Village loop from Eslida&lt;/a&gt; will take you through the historic village of Aín, known for its cultural wealth.
tips: From its 948 meters above sea level, the Puntal de l'Aljub offers very beautiful views of the Serra d'Espadà and the valleys of the La Plana region. The views may be somewhat disturbed by the antennas but you will surely find some good panoramic views!
